isro_justification: Site #1 is recommended as the primary landing target. Mean slope of 2.0° is below the 10° stability threshold (Arvidson 2002), ensuring safe touchdown and rover mobility. Mean illumination of 34.1% exceeds the 20% minimum required for continuous solar power and Earth communications (Mazarico 2011). The nearest high-confidence ice-trap region is only 2.11 km away, achievable in a single rover traverse session. The site lies adjacent to — but not inside — the deep PSR, giving access to both illuminated terrain and the cold-trap ice resource, consistent with the dual-objective design of Chandrayaan-3 and the ISRO Lunar Polar Exploration mission concept.
